title: Antipose IX
content:

{{Quote|Good evening, ladies and gentlemen, and welcome aboard the pleasure yacht ''Arleen'', now in interstellar transit from Antipose IX to—|A pilot droid|<ref name="LCMS">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]''</ref>}}
'''Antipose IX''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] celestial body located in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. The pleasure [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] ''[[Arleen]]'' made [[Standard year/Legends|year]]-long tours from the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] to Antipose IX and the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Antipose XII]].

==Description==
Antipose IX was an astronomical object located in the [[Antipose system/Legends|Antipose system]],<ref name="Gamer5" /> a part of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Along with the rest of the [[Star system/Legends|system]],<ref name="TEA" /> including its [[Antipose system star|star]],<ref name="Antipose star" /> Antipose IX was connected by the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to the [[Falko system/Legends|Falko]] and [[Paulking system/Legends|Paulking systems]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
The pleasure [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] ''[[Arleen]]'',<ref name="Gamer5" /> which existed<ref name="LCMS" /> by [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[The Lando Calrissian Adventures (omnibus)|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] novel trilogy, of which ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'' is the first installment, between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].</ref> made [[Standard year/Legends|year]]-long tours from the Oseon system to Antipose IX as well as to<ref name="Gamer5" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Antipose">{{CSWECite|I|36|Antipose}}</ref> [[Antipose XII]].<ref name="Gamer5" /> In 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> the [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] rented a [[Unidentified pilot droid (Oseon 2795)|pilot droid]] to help him travel in his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], from the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Oseon 2795]] to the planet [[Rafa IV]] in the [[Rafa system/Legends|Rafa system]]. Once the [[Light freighter/Legends|freighter]] arrived at its destination, the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] addressed a non-existent audience as if it was aboard ''Arleen'' instead and claimed that the vessel was undergoing an [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] voyage from Antipose IX. At that point, Calrissian, who had become highly annoyed with the droid, switched it off.<ref name="LCMS" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:TheMindharpofSharu.png|thumb|right|250px|Antipose IX was introduced in ''Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu''.]]
Antipose IX was first mentioned in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'', the [[1983]] first entry in [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy of novels by [[L. Neil Smith]].<ref name="LCMS" /> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed Antipose IX in the [[Seswenna sector/Legends|Seswenna sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref>

''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', released in [[2008]], also created an apparent contradiction by stating that the Antipose system, and therefore Antipose IX, was situated "not far from Centrality,"<ref name="CSWE Antipose" /> despite the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article "[[A Campaign Guide to the Centrality]]"—authored by [[Michael Kogge]] and published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 5|fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Gamer5" /> on [[July 24]], [[2001]]<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010627.html|text=''Gamer'' #5. A Domain of Evil it is.}}</ref>—previously establishing that the system indeed constituted a part of the sector.<ref name="Gamer5" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently overrode both the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement and information from ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' by placing the Antipose system in grid square U-8 as part of the Centrality.<ref name="TEA" />
